channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump challenges Biden to a cognitive test but confuses the name of the doctor who tested him
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump on Saturday night suggested President Joe Biden “should have to take a cognitive test," only to confuse who administered the test to him in the next sentence. The former president and presumptive Republican nominee referred to Texas Republican Rep.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Detroit pastor thanks Trump for visiting the 'hood' as Biden, Obama who ‘never came’ attend LA fundraiser
item.description: Detroit Pastor Lorenzo Sewell told former President Trump Saturday he was "humbled" by his visit to his church, saying Biden and Obama never visited "the hood.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump blasts immigrants for taking jobs as he courts voters at a Black church, MAGA event in Detroit
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Donald Trump blamed immigrants for stealing jobs and government resources as he courted separate groups of Black voters and hardcore conservatives in battleground Michigan on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden strikes gold in California, one week after Trump's massive haul in the blue bastion
item.description: Late-night TV host Jimmy Kimmel will interview President Biden, former President Obama, George Clooney and Julia Roberts at a star-studded $28 million fundraiser
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indiana Republicans upset Mike Braun’s choice for lieutenant governor, backing far-right pick
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Indiana Republicans rejected Sen. Mike Braun’s choice for lieutenant governor on Saturday, instead nominating a podcasting pastor with far-right views to be his running mate. Braun, who is leaving the Senate, endorsed state Rep.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump marks his 78th birthday with a towering 'MAGA' cake and attacks on his 81-year-old rival's age
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump marked his 78th birthday on Friday night by addressing a fawning crowd in Florida and repeatedly dismissing his opponent in November’s election, 81-year-old President Joe Biden, as too frail to handle a second term.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: George Clooney rubs elbows with Biden at star-studded LA fundraiser after calling White House with complaint
item.description: George Clooney will be rubbing elbows with President Biden at a fundraiser Saturday evening after calling the White House to complain about the president’s criticism of the ICC.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Obama marks 12 years since 'Dreamers' executive action, seeks 'permanent' solution for DACA recipients
item.description: Former President Obama on Saturday marked than a decade since the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals program was passed and called on Congress to codify the program into law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House GOP launches investigation into federally funded news ratings group's impact on free speech
item.description: The Oversight Committee is investigating NewsGuard's impact "on protected First Amendment speech and its potential to serve as a non-transparent agent of censorship campaigns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The US supports 'a just and lasting peace' for Ukraine, Harris tells Zelenskyy at Swiss summit
item.description: OBBÜRGEN, Switzerland (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris on Saturday pledged America's full support in backing Ukraine and global efforts to achieve “a just and lasting peace” in the face of Russia's invasion, representing the United States at an international gathering on the war and meeting with
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden, Trump step up outreach to Black men
item.description: President Biden and former President Trump are ramping up their messaging toward Black male voters, underscoring the political power the demographic will hold in November.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson says House will go to court for Biden audio after Justice Dept. refused to prosecute
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Speaker Mike Johnson said Friday that the House will go to court to enforce the subpoena against Attorney General Merrick Garland for access to President Joe Biden's special counsel audio interview, hours after the Justice Department refused to prosecute Republicans’ contempt of Co
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Health commissioner: State office dedicated to African American health ‘beacon of hope’
item.description: The Office of African American Health, established during last year’s legislative session, focuses on identifying and addressing the health disparities faced by African American communities in the state.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst, MPR News

item.title: Hulk Hogan won't rule out future in politics: 'I’ll rule with an iron fist'
item.description: The WWE Hall of Famer added his top policy positions would include "a flat tax and nothing but common sense.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: Hulk Hogan won't rule out future in politics: 'I’ll rule with an iron fist'
item.description: Wrestling legend Hulk Hogan said he'd be willing to step into the political ring if necessary, when asked during a Fox News interview Friday.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Milwaukee mayor says city is 'splendid,' day after Trump's alleged jab
item.description: Whether or not Trump called his city "horrible," Johnson said it is a place seeing massive growth and declining crime rates after the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin Republicans rush to Trump's defense after Milwaukee quip
item.description: Wisconsin Republicans rushed to former President Trump’s defense Thursday after it was reported that the presumptive GOP nominee criticized Milwaukee during a closed-door conference meeting.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada lawmakers respond to bump stock ruling; devices used in Las Vegas mass shooting
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Nevada's congressional delegation and state leaders responded Friday to the U.S. Supreme Court's 6-3 ruling striking down a Trump-era ban on bump stocks, a gun accessory restricted following the 1 October shooting.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota Chippewa Tribe general elections have concluded, new chairperson to lead Fond du Lac Band
item.description: Several band’s incumbent candidates were reelected, while the Fond du Lac Band of Lake Superior Chippewa will have a change in leadership come July with a newly elected chairperson.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: 3 newcomers, 1 incumbent elected to Fargo school board
item.description: A total of nine candidates — two incumbents and seven challengers — were seeking one of four seats up for election on the board.
item.dc:creator: Forum News Service, MPR News

item.title: Democrats favored in California's competitive races, new poll finds
item.description: A majority of California voters, including those in competitive districts currently held by a Republican, said they plan to vote for Democratic candidates in a number of federal races this November, according to a new Public Policy Institute of California poll.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down Trump-era ban on rapid-fire rifle bump stocks, reopening political fight
item.description: The Supreme Court has struck down a Trump-era ban on bump stocks, a rapid-fire gun accessory that was used in the deadliest mass shooting in modern U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gov. Hochul considers face mask ban on NYC subways
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul is pushing for a bill that could ban face masks on the subway in New York City. It's part of an effort to deter individuals who try to hide their identities while committing antisemitic acts, she said.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump on alien lifeforms: 'There could be' but 'it's not my thing'
item.description: Trump appeared on YouTuber Logan Paul's "Impaulsive," where he spent minutes discussing the possibility of life beyond Earth and whether unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs) could in fact be aliens.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida congressman to introduce bill to rename coastal waters after Donald Trump
item.description: Republican Congressman Greg Steube, whose district includes Sarasota County, will introduce legislation on Friday to rename the United State's coastal waters after former President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sen. John Fetterman was at fault in car accident and seen going 'high rate of speed,' police say
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Pennsylvania Sen. John Fetterman was seen driving at a “high rate of speed” on Sunday morning just before he rear-ended another car on Interstate 70 in Maryland, according to a state police report. The police report said Fetterman was at fault in the crash.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden campaign and Jill Biden launch outreach to older voters with bingo nights and pickleball games
item.description: First lady Jill Biden is kicking off her husband’s outreach to older voters, an effort that will blend rallies and phone banks with social events like bingo nights and pickleball games.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Poll: Majority of Americans view Kamala Harris unfavorably
item.description: Most Americans don't think Kamala Harris could win an election as the Democratic nominee, the poll found.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: California Senate approves ban on schools notifying parents of their child's pronoun change
item.description: School districts in California would be barred from forcing teachers to notify parents if their child asks to go by a new pronoun at school under a bill the state Legislature is weighing amid legal battles over the rights of parents and gender-nonconforming students.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa Bay area lost out on millions when DeSantis slashed state budget
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis slashed about $1 billion from the $116.5 billion 2024-25 budget he signed into law Wednesday, which includes millions in proposed projects for the Tampa Bay area.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 votes separate these Las Vegas valley candidates in Nevada primary
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Four votes separated the top two candidates in a North Las Vegas city council race as the county continued to tabulate primary results Wednesday. As of 1 p.m.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden administration moves to ban medical debt from credit reports
item.description: Officials said that the new rule, which will go into effect retroactively on past medical bills, will allow for stronger assessments of credit worthiness going forward.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: NYC renters, real estate agents clash ahead of broker fees hearing
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Renters and real estate agents are expected to rally in front of City Hall on Wednesday during a hearing on broker fees. New York City apartment availability is at its lowest level in 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Kirstin Cole, PIX 11

item.title: Berkley, Seaman expected to face-off in Las Vegas mayoral race
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) - Soon after the polls closed on Tuesday evening mayoral candidate Shelley Berkley started out leading the race coming in with 38% of the vote and Victoria Seaman with 27% of the vote.
item.dc:creator: Julia Romero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Latimer leads Bowman in NY-16 Democratic primary battle: PIX11 poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Two weeks before Election Day, a new poll shows Westchester County Executive George Latimer is leading incumbent U.S. Rep. Jamaal Bowman in what has become a heated primary in the Bronx and Westchester County.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: LIVE BLOG: Voters decide on key races in Nevada primary
item.description: Nevada voters will cast their ballots in the primary election today, with races for seats held by Senator Jacky Rosen and Representative Susie Lee, as well as the Las Vegas mayor's seat, which will be the first time in 25 years someone other than a Goodman will be in the mayor's office.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Culinary union calls Trump’s pledge to end taxes on tips ‘wild campaign promises’
item.description: The Culinary Union Local 226 has criticized former President Donald Trump's pledge to end taxes on tipped income as a "wild campaign promise" and called for relief for tip earners in Nevada.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News
